title: narcissus minor var. villarvildensis
intro: Narcissus minor var. villarvildensis, also known as the Dwarf Narcissus or Wild Daffodil, is a small, bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its bulbs contain alkaloids with medicinal properties, particularly galantamine, which has been found to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Narcissus minor var. villarvildensis include its potential to improve cognitive function, alleviate symptoms of dementia and Alzheimer's disease, and provide relief from anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to use the plant under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
